_comment: Trigger-eval cases for skills/classify-or-flag/SKILL.md. Each case has a prompt and a reason explaining the expected routing decision.

should_fire.prompt: Flag each construction-site image as PPE compliant or non-compliant with one whole-scene verdict
should_fire.reason: One whole-image PPE verdict is image-level classification

should_fire.prompt: Is this PCB image defective or passing quality control?
should_fire.reason: Pass/fail quality control verdict on a whole image — classic classify-or-flag trigger

should_fire.prompt: Classify each product image as good, minor defect, or reject
should_fire.reason: Multi-class image label with defect classes — image-level classification trigger

should_fire.prompt: Classify each team photo as whole-scene safety-vest compliant or non-compliant
should_fire.reason: Whole-scene compliance produces one label per image

should_fire.prompt: Build a pass/fail inspection system for our welding quality images
should_fire.reason: Pass/fail inspection on welding images — binary image-level classification trigger

should_fire.prompt: Does this whole image show an anomaly or is it normal production?
should_fire.reason: Anomaly detection at image level — anomaly is an explicit TRIGGER keyword

should_fire.prompt: I need binary classification to separate compliant vs non-compliant parts
should_fire.reason: Binary classification with compliance verdict — both keywords in TRIGGER clause

should_fire.prompt: Flag any image where the operator is not wearing gloves
should_fire.reason: PPE glove compliance — flag image-level is an explicit classify-or-flag trigger

should_not_fire.prompt: Flag every worker who is not wearing a hard hat and return one box per person
should_not_fire.reason: Per-person PPE requires instance detection and routes to detect-and-analyze

should_not_fire.prompt: Count how many defective items are visible on the production line
should_not_fire.reason: Per-instance count of defective items — routes to detect-and-analyze, not classify-or-flag

should_not_fire.prompt: Segment the defect area and measure its exact size in pixels
should_not_fire.reason: Pixel-precise segmentation mask required — routes to segment-and-analyze

should_not_fire.prompt: Track workers as they move through the facility zones
should_not_fire.reason: Identity-linked tracking across frames — routes to track-and-count

should_not_fire.prompt: Read the date code and serial number from the product label
should_not_fire.reason: Text extraction / OCR task — routes to read-text

should_not_fire.prompt: How much does Roboflow cost for my 8 inspection streams running 24/7?
should_not_fire.reason: Cost/scale question with no unsolved classification problem — routes to economics-consultant

should_not_fire.prompt: Compare self-hosting vs managed Roboflow for our production deployment
should_not_fire.reason: Build-vs-buy / deployment analysis — routes to economics-consultant

should_not_fire.prompt: I already have a trained classifier. How do I export it to ONNX?
should_not_fire.reason: User has a working model and asks only about export — out of scope per SKIP clause

should_not_fire.prompt: How many boxes and pallets are in each warehouse camera frame?
should_not_fire.reason: Per-instance count of objects per frame — routes to detect-and-analyze, not classify-or-flag
